title: Attention Is All You Need
abstract: The Transformer is a neural sequence transduction model based entirely on attention mechanisms. It became a foundation for modern language models and representation learning.

title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
abstract: ReAct interleaves reasoning traces and task-specific actions, improving language model performance on question answering and interactive decision making tasks.

title: Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools
abstract: Toolformer shows how language models can learn to call external tools using self-supervised data generation, including APIs for calculation, search, and translation.

title: SPECTER: Document-level Representation Learning using Citation-informed Transformers
abstract: SPECTER learns scientific document embeddings by training a transformer with citation graph supervision. It improves scientific paper recommendation and search.

title: A Survey of Large Language Model Based Autonomous Agents
abstract: This survey reviews autonomous agents powered by large language models, covering planning, memory, tool use, and multi-agent collaboration.

title: Automated Scientific Discovery: From Equation Discovery to Agentic Laboratories
abstract: The paper surveys computational systems for scientific discovery, including symbolic regression, experiment planning, and emerging agentic lab workflows.
